method_atom: A framework that generalizes a variational (action-based) theory by replacing a linear dependence on a fundamental scalar invariant with an arbitrary nonlinear function of that invariant, then deriving the governing equations from a stationarity principle under alternative choices of which quantities are treated as independent variables. The resulting higher-order equations are analyzed for their dynamical consequences, their equivalence to reformulated (e.g. auxiliary-field) versions of the theory, and the self-consistency/stability constraints that select admissible functions.
object_atom: The gravitational field and spacetime geometry as described by general relativity — the curvature invariant of spacetime and its behavior across cosmological and astrophysical regimes, i.e. modifications and extensions of Einstein's theory of gravity.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is generalizing an action by an arbitrary nonlinear function of a core invariant plus variational derivation and viability/equivalence analysis; what is domain-specific is that the invariant and dynamics are those of spacetime curvature/gravity, with no unavoidable fusion between the two.
